claim: Step 1 enumerates 48552 primitive projective 5-tuples (a2,a1,a0,b1,b0) satisfying 9*a2+2*b1^2=0, |a1|,|a0|,|b0|<=8, b1 in {3,6,...,30}, gcd=1, b1>0, b1+b0!=0.
Stratified random sample of 40 distinct-L groups per b1 bucket (10 buckets) yields 400 PCFs evaluated at dps=120/400. Seed=42.
Two-stage PSLQ with phantom-hit rejection (rel[0]=0 -> Trans) and rational pre-check classifies the 400-sample as Trans=387, Log=0, Alg=0, Rat=13.
No phantom hits (PSLQ relations with L-coefficient 0) survived classification.
Every b1 bucket in {3,6,9,12,15,18,21,24,27,30} contains >=32 Trans families in the 40-element stratified sample; minimum bucket is b1=3 with 32 Trans + 8 Rat.

UMB Section 4 Remark 8 verdict: CONFIRMED-BY-EXAMPLE. The Trans-stratum at a2/b1^2=-2/9 is non-empty in projective coordinates across all 3|b1 slices examined; the P-04 affine null was a coordinate artefact.
